sections.label: Section 1.1320
sections.original: διπλᾶ σε πενθεῖν καὶ διπλᾶ φορεῖν κακά .
sections.translation: you mourn and bear a double pain .

sections.label: Section 1.1321
sections.original: ἰὼ φίλος ,
sections.translation: Ah , friend , you still are steadfast in your care for me , and still have patience to tend to the blind man ! Ah , me !

sections.label: Section 1.1325
sections.original: οὐ γάρ με λήθεις , ἀλλὰ γιγνώσκω σαφῶς ,
sections.translation: Your presence is not hidden from me — no , blind though I am , nevertheless I know your voice full well .

sections.label: Section 1.1327
sections.original: ὦ δεινὰ δράσας , πῶς ἔτλης τοιαῦτα σὰς
sections.translation: Man of dread deeds , how could you quench your vision in this way ? What divinity urged you on ?

sections.label: Section 2.1329
sections.original: Ἀπόλλων τάδʼ ἦν , Ἀπόλλων , φίλοι ,
sections.translation: It was Apollo , friends , Apollo who brought these troubles

sections.label: Section 2.1330
sections.original: ὁ κακὰ κακὰ τελῶν ἐμὰ τάδʼ ἐμὰ πάθεα .
sections.translation: to pass , these terrible , terrible troubles . But the hand that struck my eyes was none other than my own , wretched that I am !

sections.label: Section 2.1335
sections.original: ὅτῳ γʼ ὁρῶντι μηδὲν ἦν ἰδεῖν γλυκύ ;
sections.translation: Why should I see , when sight showed me nothing sweet ?

sections.label: Section 2.1336
sections.original: ἦν τᾷδʼ ὅπωσπερ καὶ σύ φῄς .
sections.translation: These things were just as you say .

sections.label: Section 2.1337
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἐμοὶ βλεπτὸν ἢ
sections.translation: What , my friends , can I behold anymore , what can I love , what greeting can touch my ear with joy ? Hurry , friends ,

sections.label: Section 2.1340
sections.original: ἀπάγετʼ ἐκτόπιον ὅ τι τάχιστά με ,
sections.translation: lead me from the land , lead me from here , the utterly lost ,

sections.label: Section 2.1345
sections.original: ἐχθρότατον βροτῶν .
sections.translation: the thrice-accursed , the mortal most hateful to the gods !

sections.label: Section 2.1347
sections.original: ὡς σʼ ἠθέλησα μηδέ γʼ ἂν γνῶναί ποτε .
sections.translation: Wretched alike for your fortune and for your understanding of it , would that I had never known you !

sections.label: Section 2.1349
sections.original: ὄλοιθʼ ὅστις ἦν , ὃς ἀγρίας πέδας
sections.translation: Perish the man , whoever he was , who freed me in the past years from the cruel shackle on my feet — a thankless deed ! Had I died then ,

sections.label: Section 2.1355
sections.original: οὐκ ἦ φίλοισιν οὐδʼ ἐμοὶ τοσόνδʼ ἄχος .
sections.translation: I would not have been so sore a grief to my friends and to my own soul .

sections.label: Section 2.1356
sections.original: θέλοντι κἀμοὶ τοῦτʼ ἂν ἦν .
sections.translation: I too would have had it thus .

sections.label: Section 2.1357
sections.original: οὔκουν πατρός γʼ ἂν φονεὺς
sections.translation: In this way I would not have come to shed my father ’ s blood , or been known among men as the husband of the woman from whom I was born .

sections.label: Section 2.1360
sections.original: νῦν δʼ ἄθεος μέν εἰμʼ , ἀνοσίων δὲ παῖς ,
sections.translation: Now I am forsaken by the gods , son of a defiled mother , successor to the bed of the man who gave me my own wretched being :

sections.label: Section 2.1365
sections.original: εἰ δέ τι πρεσβύτερον ἔτι κακοῦ κακόν ,
sections.translation: if there is a woe surpassing all woes , it has become Oedipus ’ lot .

sections.label: Section 1367
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ ὅπως σε φῶ βεβουλεῦσθαι καλῶς ·
sections.translation: I cannot agree that you have counseled well : you would have been better dead than living and blind .

sections.label: Section 1369
sections.original: ὡς μὲν τάδʼ οὐχ ὧδʼ ἔστʼ ἄριστʼ εἰργασμένα ,
sections.translation: Do not tell me that things have not been best done in this way :

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: μή μʼ ἐκδίδασκε , μηδὲ συμβούλευʼ ἔτι .
sections.translation: give me counsel no more . If I had sight , I know not with what eyes I could even have looked on my father , when I came to the house of Hades , or on my miserable mother , since against both I have sinned such sins as hanging myself could not punish .

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἡ τέκνων δῆτʼ ὄψις ἦν ἐφίμερος ,
sections.translation: But do you think that the sight of children , born as mine were , was lovely for me to look upon ? No , no , never lovely to my eyes ! No , neither was this town with its towering walls , nor the sacred statues of the gods , since I , thrice wretched that I am —

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: κάλλιστʼ ἀνὴρ εἷς ἔν γε ταῖς Θήβαις τραφεὶς
sections.translation: I , noblest of the sons of Thebes — have doomed myself to know them no more by commanding that all should reject the impious one , the one whom the gods have revealed as unholy , a member of Laius ’ own race ! After bearing such a stain upon myself ,

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: ὀρθοῖς ἔμελλον ὄμμασιν τούτους ὁρᾶν ;
sections.translation: was I to look with steady eyes on this folk ? No indeed : were there a way to choke the source of hearing , I would not have hesitated to make a fast prison of this wretched frame , so that I should have known neither sight nor sound .

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: τὴν φροντίδʼ ἔξω τῶν κακῶν οἰκεῖν γλυκύ .
sections.translation: It is sweet for our thought to dwell beyond the sphere of grief . Alas , Cithaeron , why did you provide a shelter for me ? When I was given to you , why did you not slay me straightway , that I might never reveal my origin to men . Ah , Polybus , ah , Corinth , and you that were called the ancient house of my father ,

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.original: λόγῳ παλαιὰ δώμαθʼ , οἷον ἆρά με
sections.translation: how fair-seeming was I , your nurseling , and what evils were festering underneath ! Now I am found to be evil and of evil birth . Oh you three roads , and you secret glen , you , thicket , and narrow way where three paths met —

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: αἳ τοὐμὸν αἷμα τῶν ἐμῶν χειρῶν ἄπο
sections.translation: you who drank my father ’ s blood from my own hands — do you remember , perhaps , what deeds I have performed in your sight , and then what fresh deeds I went on to do when I came here ? Oh marriage rites , you gave me birth , and when you had brought me forth ,

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: ἀνεῖτε ταὐτοῦ σπέρμα , κἀπεδείξατε
sections.translation: you again bore children to your child , you created an incestuous kinship of fathers , brothers , sons , brides , wives , and mothers — all the foulest deeds that are wrought among men ! But it is improper to mention what it is improper to do —

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.original: ὅπως τάχιστα πρὸς θεῶν ἔξω μέ που
sections.translation: hurry , for the love of the gods , hide me somewhere beyond the land , or slay me , or cast me into the sea , where you will never behold me any longer ! Approach — deign to lay your hands on a wretched man — listen and fear not : my plague can rest

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.original: οὐδεὶς οἷός τε πλὴν ἐμοῦ φέρειν βροτῶν .
sections.translation: on no other mortal .

sections.label: Section 1416
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὧν ἐπαιτεῖς εἰς δέον πάρεσθʼ ὅδε
sections.translation: But here is Creon in good time to plan and perform that which you request . He alone is left to guard the land in your place .

sections.label: Section 1419
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί δῆτα λέξομεν πρὸς τόνδʼ ἔπος ;
sections.translation: Ah , me , how will I address him ?

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: τίς μοι φανεῖται πίστις ἔνδικος ; τὰ γὰρ
sections.translation: What claim to credence can be shown on my part ? For in the past I proved to be wholly false to him .

sections.label: Section 1422
sections.original: οὐχ ὡς γελαστής , Οἰδίπους , ἐλήλυθα ,
sections.translation: I have not come to mock or reproach you with any any past fault .

sections.label: Section 1424
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ τὰ θνητῶν μὴ καταισχύνεσθʼ ἔτι
sections.translation: But you , if you no longer respect the children of men ,

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.original: γένεθλα , τὴν γοῦν πάντα βόσκουσαν φλόγα
sections.translation: revere at least the all-nurturing flame of our lord the Sun , and don ’ t show so openly such a pollution as this , one which neither earth , nor holy rain , nor the light itself can welcome . Take him into the house as quickly as you can : it best accords with pity that

sections.label: Section 1430
sections.original: τοῖς ἐν γένει γὰρ τἀγγενῆ μάλισθʼ ὁρᾶν
sections.translation: kinfolk alone should see and hear a kinsman ’ s woes .

sections.label: Section 1432
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν , ἐπείπερ ἐλπίδος μʼ ἀπέσπασας ,
sections.translation: For the gods ’ love — since you have done a gentle violence to my prediction and come in a spirit so noble to me , a man most vile — grant me a favor : I will speak for your own good , not mine .

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: καὶ τοῦ με χρείας ὧδε λιπαρεῖς τυχεῖν ;
sections.translation: And what do you wish so eagerly to get from me ?

sections.label: Section 1436
sections.original: ῥῖψόν με γῆς ἐκ τῆσδʼ ὅσον τάχισθʼ , ὅπου
sections.translation: Cast me out of this land with all speed , to a place where no mortal shall be found to greet me .

sections.label: Section 1438
sections.original: ἔδρασʼ ἂν εὖ τοῦτʼ ἴσθʼ ἄν , εἰ μὴ τοῦ θεοῦ
sections.translation: This I could have done , to be sure , except I craved first to learn from the god all my duty .

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἥ γʼ ἐκείνου πᾶσʼ ἐδηλώθη φάτις ,
sections.translation: But his pronouncement has been set forth in full — to let me perish , the parricide , unholy one that I am .

sections.label: Section 1442
sections.original: οὕτως ἐλέχθη ταῦθʼ · ὅμως δʼ ἵνʼ ἕσταμεν
sections.translation: Thus it was said . But since we have come to such a pass , it is better to learn clearly what should be done .

sections.label: Section 1444
sections.original: οὕτως ἄρʼ ἀνδρὸς ἀθλίου πεύσεσθʼ ὕπερ ;
sections.translation: Will you , then , seek a response on behalf of such a wretch as I ?

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ σὺ νῦν τἂν τῷ θεῷ πίστιν φέροις .
sections.translation: Yes , for even you yourself will now surely put faith in the god .

sections.label: Section 1446
sections.original: καὶ σοί γʼ ἐπισκήπτω τε καὶ προστέψομαι ,
sections.translation: Yes . And on you I lay this charge , to you I make this entreaty : give to the woman within such burial as you wish — you will properly render the last rites to your own . But never let this city of my father be condemned

sections.label: Section 1450
sections.original: πατρῷον ἄστυ ζῶντος οἰκητοῦ τυχεῖν ,
sections.translation: to have me dwelling within , as long as I live . No , allow me to live in the hills , where Cithaeron , famed as mine , sits , which my mother and father , while they lived , fixed as my appointed tomb , so that I may die according to the decree of those who sought to slay me .

sections.label: Section 1455
sections.original: καίτοι τοσοῦτόν γʼ οἶδα , μήτε μʼ ἂν νόσον
sections.translation:
And yet I know this much , that neither sickness nor anything else can destroy me ; for I would never have been snatched from death , except in order to suffer some strange doom .
But let my fate go where it will . Regarding my children , Creon , I beg you to take no care of my sons :

sections.label: Section 1460
sections.original: προσθῇ μέριμναν · ἄνδρες εἰσίν , ὥστε μὴ
sections.translation: they are men , so that they will never lack the means to live wherever they should be . My two girls , poor hapless ones — who never knew my table spread separately , or lacked their father ’ s presence , but always had a share of all that

sections.label: Section 1465
sections.original: ψαύοιμι , πάντων τῶνδʼ ἀεὶ μετειχέτην ·
sections.translation: reached my hands — I implore you to take care of them . And , if you can , allow me to touch them with my hands , and to indulge my grief . Grant it , prince , grant it , noble heart . Ah , if I could but once touch them with my hands , I would think that I had them

sections.label: Section 1470
sections.original: δοκοῖμʼ ἔχειν σφᾶς , ὥσπερ ἡνίκʼ ἔβλεπον .
sections.translation: just as when I had sight .

sections.label: Section 1471
sections.original: τί φημί ;
sections.translation: What is this ? Oh , gods , can it be my loved ones that I hear sobbing , can Creon have taken pity on me and sent my children , my darlings ?

sections.label: Section 1475
sections.original: λέγω τι ;
sections.translation: Am I right ?

sections.label: Section 1476
sections.original: λέγεις · ἐγὼ γὰρ εἰμʼ ὁ πορσύνας τάδε ,
sections.translation: You are . I have brought this about , for I knew the joy which you have long had from them — the joy you now have .

sections.label: Section 1478
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὐτυχοίης , καί σε τῆσδε τῆς ὁδοῦ
sections.translation: Bless you , and for this errand may the god prove a kinder guardian to you than he has to me .

sections.label: Section 1480
sections.original: ὦ τέκνα , ποῦ ποτʼ ἐστέ ; δεῦρʼ ἴτʼ , ἔλθετε
sections.translation: My children , where are you ? Come , here , here to the hands of the one whose mother was your own , the hands that have made your father ’ s once bright eyes to be such orbs as these — his , who seeing nothing , knowing nothing ,

sections.label: Section 1485
sections.original: πατὴρ ἐφάνθην ἔνθεν αὐτὸς ἠρόθην .
sections.translation: became your father by her from whom he was born ! For you also do I weep , though I cannot see you , when I think of the bitter life that men will make you live in days to come . To what company of the citizens will you go , to what festival ,

sections.label: Section 1490
sections.original: ποίας δʼ ἑορτάς , ἔνθεν οὐ κεκλαυμέναι
sections.translation: from which you will not return home in tears , not sharing in the holiday ? But when you reach a ripe age for marriage , who shall he be , who shall be the man , my daughters , to risk taking upon himself the reproaches

sections.label: Section 1495
sections.original: γοναῖσιν ἔσται σφῷν θʼ ὁμοῦ δηλήματα ;
sections.translation: that will certainly be baneful to my offspring and yours ? What misery is lacking ? Your father killed his own father , and bore you from the source of his own being !

sections.label: Section 1500
sections.original: τοιαῦτʼ ὀνειδιεῖσθε · κᾆτα τίς γαμεῖ ;
sections.translation: Such are the taunts that will be cast at you . And who then will wed you ? The man does not live , no , it cannot be , my children , but you will wither in barren maidenhood . Son of Menoeceus , hear me : since you are the only father left to them — we , their parents , are both gone —
